commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c0dfd697709b515b9912a7675c81a5ec9cef793b)
commands.review_summary:
This PR caps embedded-agent compaction reserve and keep-recent token budgets against known model context windows, updates focused tests, and updates config help/type/gateway docs plus generated config hashes.

PR surface: Source +15, Tests +33, Docs +2, Generated 0. Total +50 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level reproduction is high confidence: current main leaves 16,384 reserve and 20,000 keep-recent values in place for a 16K context, while the PR tests assert capped 8,384 and 8,000 values. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow docs repair is suitable for automation; maintainer review still owns the compatibility tradeoff.

target.title: fix(agents): cap compaction budgets for small contexts
target.body:
## Summary

- cap embedded-agent compaction reserve when the known model context window would otherwise leave no usable prompt budget
- cap keep-recent tokens to the prompt budget remaining after reserve for small and mid-sized contexts
- update config help/types, generated config baseline hash, and gateway config docs to document context-window caps

## Verification

Behavior addressed: small-context runs no longer retain impossible compaction `reserveTokens` or `keepRecentTokens` budgets when `contextTokenBudget` is known.

Real environment tested: focused local Vitest/docs/format proof in a Codex worktree, branch autoreview, and AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-settings.test.ts --reporter=dot
node --import tsx scripts/generate-config-doc-baseline.ts
node --import tsx scripts/generate-config-doc-baseline.ts --check
node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/gateway/config-agents.md
node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/gateway/config-agents.md
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/agent-settings.ts src/agents/agent-settings.test.ts src/config/schema.help.ts src/config/types.agent-defaults.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/agent-settings.ts src/agents/agent-settings.test.ts src/config/schema.help.ts src/config/types.agent-defaults.ts
git diff --check
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main
node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"
```

Evidence after fix: refreshed head `c0dfd697709`; focused local wrapper passed `src/agents/agent-settings.test.ts` with 36/36 tests; generated config baseline check, docs MDX, docs formatting, oxfmt, oxlint, and diff checks passed; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings and `overall: patch is correct (0.78)`. AWS Crabbox `check:changed` passed with provider `aws`, lease `cbx_d6af1e4801b7`, slug `tidal-hermit`, run `run_66057c4bc436`, machine `c7a.8xlarge`, exit 0, and lease cleanup stopped; the changed gate selected lanes `core`, `coreTests`, and `docs`.

Observed result after fix: a 16K-context profile resolves reserve to `8,384` and keep-recent to `8,000`; configured over-budget reserve and keep-recent values are capped to the available context window; when no context window is known, existing uncapped behavior is preserved.

What was not tested: no live Ollama generation for this patch; the changed behavior is the embedded-agent settings normalization layer and is covered by focused settings tests, branch review, and remote changed-gate proof.
target.author: vincentkoc
